reasoning: The Bank Prime Loan Rate only changes when the Federal Reserve changes its benchmark Fed Funds rate, which typically only occurs during scheduled FOMC meetings. The 2026 FOMC meeting schedule confirms that meetings are set for March 17-18 and April 28-29, meaning there is no scheduled meeting between March 29 and April 5. Furthermore, the rate has been steadily decreasing over the last year (from 7.5 to 6.75), making an emergency, inter-meeting rate hike extremely improbable. Therefore, the chance of an increase in this exact 7-day window is very low.
